shortDescription.text: The right SHIELDS panel continues beyond the right frame edge and its label is rendered in very low-contrast gray, while the left SCORE panel is fully visible and bright.

help.text: Anchor both top HUD panels to a shared safe-area container, clamp the right edge, and use the approved high-contrast text token; verify at 16:9, ultrawide, and reduced-width resolutions.

shortDescription.text: A bright magenta rectangle and the text 'STALE DEBUG BOUNDS' remain over the central gameplay object in the final frame.

help.text: Gate diagnostic rendering behind development-build flags, clear overlay state on scene transitions, and add a release-capture check that rejects debug-layer colors/labels.

shortDescription.text: The SCORE panel shifts downward relative to the previous frame, and the MISSION READY panel shifts left and downward while the center object and SHIELDS panel remain stable.

help.text: Use fixed safe-area anchors and shared layout constraints for persistent HUD elements; add screenshot assertions for panel bounds across stable gameplay frames.

message.text:
The right SHIELDS panel continues beyond the right frame edge and its label is rendered in very low-contrast gray, while the left SCORE panel is fully visible and bright.
Expected: The gameplay HUD should remain inside the 1280×720 safe area, use consistent panel geometry, and keep critical shield text readable.

A bright magenta rectangle and the text 'STALE DEBUG BOUNDS' remain over the central gameplay object in the final frame.
Expected: A player-facing gameplay frame should not expose editor/debug overlays, and debug labels should never occlude the focal object.

The SCORE panel shifts downward relative to the previous frame, and the MISSION READY panel shifts left and downward while the center object and SHIELDS panel remain stable.
Expected: Persistent HUD panels should keep stable anchors and baselines across adjacent frames unless an intentional animation is visible.
